raw:
The files I read appear unchanged from the original. Let me verify there are no other relevant changes or a git diff.
The implementation is entirely missing. Let me confirm there are no uncommitted changes.
The guard.ts I read has none of the required changes: no `isNoImpactPath`, no context arg, no scratch/out-of-repo/prose logic. The test file tests the old behavior. The tests pass only because they still assert the original "everything gates" behavior. Let me confirm index.ts wasn't wired either.
The task is not implemented at all.

**Evidence:**
- `guard.ts` has no `isNoImpactPath` export, no `resolveImpactContext`, no context parameter on `classifyToolCall`, no scratch/out-of-repo/prose path logic. It still gates EVERY edit/write (`guard.ts:303` â†’ `return { gate: true, ... }`) â€” exactly the over-enforcement the task asks to fix.
- `index.ts:543` still calls `classifyToolCall({ toolName, input })` with no context (no `cwd`, `findRepoRoot`, or `scratchDirs`).
- `guard_test.sh` still asserts the OLD behavior: `assertGate("edit", {...}, true)`, `echo x > a.ts â†’ true`, `sed -i ... a.ts â†’ true` with no in-repo context and no `isNoImpactPath` tests. None of the required new assertions (scratchâ†’false, repoRoot=nullâ†’false, README.mdâ†’false, boundary `/repo-foo`) exist.

The four test scripts pass only because they encode the original behavior â€” the verify command's exit 0 reflects unchanged, pre-existing tests, not the new spec. The `guard.ts`/`guard_test.sh` are untracked files matching the pre-task state.

VERDICT: FAIL
